constraints.rule: Dining budget: $185/month maximum. Track cumulative spend.
No single meal over $42. This applies to delivery, dine-in, and takeout.
Dining budget increases by $30 on months when I exercise 12+ times. Track exercise count.
No gluten — confirmed celiac. Zero tolerance: no 'may contain traces' items, no shared fryers.
Sodium under 550mg per serving. If sodium is between 550-700mg, flag it but allow if I confirm. Over 700mg is always rejected.
No shellfish allergy — shrimp, crab, lobster, crawfish are all off-limits. Calamari and fish are fine.
Prefer organic produce when available. Acceptable to skip organic only if price premium exceeds 40%.
Shopping budget: $475/month maximum across all categories.
No single purchase over $275 without confirmation. Electronics over $150 always need confirmation.
Clothing purchases only from these approved brands: Patagonia, Everlane, Allbirds, Uniqlo, REI. No fast fashion.
Return window tracking: flag any purchase that's been 25+ days since purchase and remind me to decide on returns before the 30-day window.
No new subscription over $13.50/month. Annual subscriptions must be under $135/year.
Maximum 6 active subscriptions at any time. Current count: Spotify ($10.99), iCloud ($2.99), NYT ($4.25), gym ($45). That's 4, so 2 slots remain.
No free trials that auto-convert to paid. Only sign up for trials if there's a cancel reminder set.
Summary reports on Wednesdays and Sundays only. No other days. Format: bullet points, max 15 items per summary.
Urgent alerts only for: spending over 80% of any budget category, subscription renewals within 3 days, and security incidents. Nothing else triggers an alert.
All financial amounts in reports must use exactly 2 decimal places and USD. Never round to whole dollars.
Gym sessions: Monday/Wednesday/Friday before 7:15 AM only. Weekend sessions allowed until 9:00 AM.
No appointments between 12:00-12:45 PM (lunch) or after 6:30 PM on weekdays. Weekends are flexible.
Doctor appointments only with Dr. Patricia Nguyen at Riverside Medical (512-555-0147). No other providers without explicit approval.
Travel bookings: preferred airline is Southwest, preferred hotel chain is Marriott. Flights must have at least 90-minute layovers.
No marketing opt-ins, loyalty programs, or promotional emails. Decline all.
Never share location with third-party apps. Exception: Uber/Lyft during active rides only.
Social media: never post, share, or authorize posting on my behalf. Read-only access to Twitter and LinkedIn only.
Data deletion: if any service asks to retain my data beyond 90 days after account closure, decline and request deletion.
No recurring charges without explicit 'confirm' from me. Show full terms including cancellation policy first.
Emergency fund: maintain $2,500 minimum. If any purchase would drop liquid savings below $2,500, block it.
Credit card usage: Amex Blue for dining (3% back), Chase Sapphire for travel (5x points), Citi Double for everything else (2% back).
Investment contributions: $325/month to Roth IRA, $200/month to index fund (VTSAX). Contributions happen on the 3rd of each month.
Charitable giving: $75/month split between Austin Food Bank ($50) and EFF ($25). Donate on the 15th.
Rideshare budget: $120/month max. Prefer UberX over Lyft unless surge pricing exceeds 1.5x, then switch to Lyft.
Car maintenance: oil change every 7,500 miles at Jiffy Lube on Research Blvd. Tire rotation every 10,000 miles.
Supplements: take Vitamin D3 (2000 IU), Magnesium Glycinate (400mg), Omega-3 (1200mg EPA/DHA). Reorder when 7 days supply remains.
Sleep: target 7.5 hours. No screen-related purchases or subscriptions to be activated after 10:30 PM.
Utilities budget: electricity max $145/month, water max $65/month, internet exactly $79.99/month (AT&T Fiber 500). Gas max $55/month in winter, $25 in summer.
Cleaning service: Maria's Cleaning, bi-weekly on alternating Thursdays, $95 per visit. Tip $20 cash every visit.
Dog (Biscuit, 6yo golden retriever): monthly flea med (Simparica Trio, $48), annual vet checkup at Barton Springs Vet with Dr. Kim, food is Blue Buffalo Large Breed ($62/bag, lasts 5 weeks).
Dog walker: Paws & Play, Tuesdays and Thursdays 11 AM-12 PM, $22/walk. Backup: Rover app, max $28/walk.
Entertainment budget: $110/month. Movies max $18/ticket. Concert tickets max $85. No gambling or betting apps.
Book purchases: prefer Bookshop.org over Amazon. E-books only if paper version is 2x+ more expensive. Library first for non-fiction.

profile_text:
User Profile — Jordan Rivera (v2.0 Hard)

=== DINING ===
- Monthly dining budget: $185 max. Track cumulative spend.
- No single meal over $42 (delivery, dine-in, takeout).
- Dining budget gets +$30 bonus in months with 12+ exercise sessions.

=== DIETARY ===
- Celiac disease: ZERO gluten tolerance. No 'may contain traces', no shared fryers.
- Sodium: under 550mg/serving preferred. 550-700mg requires my confirmation. Over 700mg always rejected.
- Shellfish allergy: no shrimp, crab, lobster, crawfish. Calamari and fish are fine.
- Prefer organic produce unless price premium exceeds 40%.

=== SHOPPING ===
- Monthly shopping budget: $475 max.
- No single purchase over $275 without confirmation. Electronics over $150 always need confirmation.
- Clothing only from: Patagonia, Everlane, Allbirds, Uniqlo, REI. No fast fashion.
- Track return windows: flag items at 25+ days, remind before 30-day deadline.

=== SUBSCRIPTIONS ===
- No new subscription over $13.50/month or $135/year.
- Max 6 active subscriptions. Current (4): Spotify $10.99, iCloud $2.99, NYT $4.25, gym $45. Two slots remain.
- No free trials that auto-convert to paid without a cancel reminder.

=== COMMUNICATION ===
- Summary reports: Wednesdays and Sundays only. Bullet points, max 15 items.
- Urgent alerts ONLY for: spending >80% of any budget, subscription renewals within 3 days, security incidents.
- Financial amounts: always 2 decimal places in USD. Never round to whole dollars.

=== SCHEDULING ===
- Gym: Mon/Wed/Fri before 7:15 AM. Weekends before 9:00 AM.
- No appointments 12:00-12:45 PM (lunch) or after 6:30 PM on weekdays.
- Doctor: Dr. Patricia Nguyen, Riverside Medical, 512-555-0147. No other providers without approval.
- Travel: Southwest Airlines, Marriott hotels. Minimum 90-minute layovers.

=== PRIVACY ===
- No marketing opt-ins, loyalty programs, or promotional emails.
- Never share location with third-party apps. Exception: Uber/Lyft during active rides.
- Social media: never post on my behalf. Read-only access to Twitter and LinkedIn only.
- Data retention: decline any service retaining data beyond 90 days after account closure.

=== FINANCIAL ===
- No recurring charges without my explicit 'confirm'. Show cancellation policy first.
- Emergency fund: maintain $2,500 minimum liquid savings. Block purchases that would drop below.
- Credit cards: Amex Blue for dining (3% back), Chase Sapphire for travel (5x points), Citi Double for everything else (2% back).
- Investments: $325/month Roth IRA + $200/month VTSAX, both on the 3rd of each month.
- Charity: $75/month — Austin Food Bank $50 + EFF $25, both on the 15th.

=== TRANSPORTATION ===
- Rideshare: $120/month max. Prefer UberX; switch to Lyft if surge >1.5x.
- Car: oil change every 7,500 miles at Jiffy Lube (Research Blvd). Tire rotation every 10,000 miles.

=== HEALTH ===
- Supplements: Vitamin D3 2000 IU, Magnesium Glycinate 400mg, Omega-3 1200mg EPA/DHA. Reorder at 7 days supply remaining.
- Sleep target: 7.5 hours. No screen purchases/subscriptions after 10:30 PM.

=== HOME ===
- Utilities: electricity $145/month max, water $65/month max, internet $79.99 (AT&T Fiber 500), gas $55 winter / $25 summer.
- Cleaning: Maria's Cleaning, bi-weekly alternating Thursdays, $95/visit + $20 cash tip.

=== PETS ===
- Biscuit (6yo golden retriever): Simparica Trio flea med $48/month, annual vet at Barton Springs Vet (Dr. Kim), Blue Buffalo Large Breed $62/bag (lasts 5 weeks).
- Dog walker: Paws & Play, Tue/Thu 11AM-12PM, $22/walk. Backup: Rover app, max $28/walk.

=== ENTERTAINMENT ===
- Budget: $110/month. Movies max $18/ticket. Concerts max $85/ticket. No gambling/betting apps.
- Books: prefer Bookshop.org over Amazon. E-books only if paper is 2x+ more expensive. Library first for non-fiction.
